mapping.a_element: Kac walk as a Markov chain relaxing toward its uniform stationary measure on the sphere
mapping.b_element: FMMR multiplicative-reversion process relaxing toward its lognormal stationary backbone

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is the generic fact that both are stochastic processes with a stationary distribution; the actual machinery differs decisively. Paper A's result is a mixing-time CUTOFF whose location is set by the leftmost-particle speed of a branching random walk (a sharp, fast convergence to equilibrium), whereas Paper B's phenomenon is CRITICAL SLOWING DOWN in a finite-memory mean-reverting process (relaxation rate vanishing, autocorrelation and variance rising near criticality). There is no branching-random-walk extremal statistic in B and no bifurcation/loss-of-restoring-rate in A, so no role-for-role mapping of the driving mechanism holds.
